OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 30, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: A. THE PRAYER FOR OVER $25,000. IS DENIED. THERE IS NOTHING IMPROPER AS TO THIS REQUEST; B. THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING TREE TOPPING AND ILLEGALITY IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND SO AS TO CONFORM WITH THE DEMURRER RULING; C. THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING INTRUSION IS DENIED; D. THE PRAYER FOR ACTUAL DAMAGES IS DENIED; E. THE PRAYER FOR TREE REPLACEMENT IS DENIED. THE PRAYER IS NOT NECESSARILY IMPROPER BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING RESTORATION; F. THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. THE FACTS SUPPORT THE CLAIM; G. THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE A STATUTORY OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR FEES. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TOM MYERS AND ANGEL LI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted and denied as follows:
A. The prayer for over $25,000. Denied. There is nothing improper as to this request; B. The allegations regarding tree topping and illegality. Granted with leave to amend so as to conform with the demurrer ruling; C. The allegations regarding intrusion. Denied; D. The prayer for actual damages. Denied; E. The prayer for tree replacement. Denied. The prayer is not necessarily improper because plaintiff is seeking restoration; F. The prayer for punitive damages. Denied. The facts support the claim; G. The prayer for attorney's fees. Granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite a statutory or contractual basis for fees. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS TOM MYERS AND ANGEL LI DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT, the Court sustains the demurrer to the neg per se cause of action without leave to amend. First, sec. 810A(a) of the SF Public Works Code requires the tree to be within 10 feet of a public right-of-way for the permit requirement to apply. Plaintiff admits that the trees that fall under the ordinance were not harmed. (Plaintiff's Opposition to the Demurrer, 5:12-20). Second, neg per se is an evidentiary presumption, not a distinct c/a. See Das v. Bank of America, N.A., (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 737-738. The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress and intrusion causes of action. Those claims involve factual issues and defendant fails to show that they fail as a matter of law on demurrer. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. First, the claim is negligence and negligent infliction of emotional distress is technically not a separate cause of action. See Potter v. Firestone Tire and Rubber. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984. Second, plaintiff cannot recover emotional distress damages on this claim because there was no physical injury or preexisting relationship between the parties to warrant that relief. See Cooper v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1012. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2014 Continued To Jan-28-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TOM MYERS AND ANGEL LI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to January 28, 2015 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 30, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
